title: use `var_samp` as window function cause unexpected error
description:
## Bug Report
When used with the OVER clause, `var_samp` functions as a window function. When there are non-aggregate columns present, it should not throw an error. 
MySQL does not throw an error, and TiDB also does not throw errors for functions like `var_pop` and `variance`.

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)
```sql
create table t1 (c1 int);
select c1, var_samp(c1) over (partition by c1) from t1;
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)
```
Empty set (0.00 sec)
```
### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
```
ERROR 8123 (HY000): In aggregated query without GROUP BY, expression #1 of SELECT list contains nonaggregated column 'c1'; this is incompatible with sql_mode=only_full_group_by
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
```
Release Version: v8.0.0
Edition: Community
Git Commit Hash: 8ba1fa452b1ccdbfb85879ea94b9254aabba2916
Git Branch: HEAD
UTC Build Time: 2024-03-28 14:22:15
GoVersion: go1.21.4
Race Enabled: false
Check Table Before Drop: false
Store: tikv
```
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2 (c1 INTEGER, c2 TEXT, c3 REAL, c4 DATE);
INSERT INTO t2 (c1, c2, c3, c4) VALUES (1, 'test', 10.50, '2024-01-01');
SELECT c1, (SELECT AVG((c3 - (SELECT AVG(c3) FROM t2 WHERE c1 = t.c1)) * (c3 - (SELECT AVG(c3) FROM t2 WHERE c1 = t.c1))) FROM t2 t WHERE c1 = t2.c1) AS var_samp_c3 FROM t2;
SELECT c1, c2, (SELECT AVG((c3 - (SELECT AVG(c3) FROM t2 WHERE c2 = t.c2)) * (c3 - (SELECT AVG(c3) FROM t2 WHERE c2 = t.c2))) FROM t2 t WHERE c2 = t2.c2) AS var_samp_c3 FROM t2;
SELECT c1, (SELECT AVG((c3 - (SELECT AVG(c3) FROM t2 WHERE c1 = t.c1)) * (c3 - (SELECT AVG(c3) FROM t2 WHERE c1 = t.c1))) FROM t2 t WHERE c1 = t2.c1) AS var_samp_c3 FROM t2 ORDER BY c4;
key_bug_features: var_samp with OVER clause throws error on non-aggregates
